### Source Citations Documented

| Claim | Source | Confidence |
|-------|--------|------------|
| Autistic brains don't habituate to sensory input (fMRI evidence) | Green et al. 2015, JAMA Psychiatry | HIGH |
| Interoception CAN be trained — 8-week structured practice | Hample, Mahler & Amspacher 2020, N=8 pilot | MEDIUM |
| 49 studies confirm interoception differences in autism | Barmpagiannis & Baldimtsi 2025, systematic review | HIGH |
| 82% showed clearer awareness in 2-4 weeks with consistent wear | Internal data, N=672 (self-reported) | LOW (internal) |
| Kelly Mahler: interoception as 8th sensory system | Mahler 2016, AAPC Publishing | HIGH (framework) |
| L2 profile: BCBA-compatible framing, prompt-dependency, mechanism-first | S2 §3.1, 15-level-messaging-matrix.md §3 | Strategy reference |

why.raw:
label: "Why we chose this story",
      body: "Sarah isn't our best case. She's our most common one. Her situation is close to what many families find — a child on the spectrum, a couple of years of tried methods, a low point that felt like an ending, and then a method that finally matched the map.",

label: "What's covered",

        "Your child refusing to wear them — full refund.",
        "No visible progress in 60 days — full refund.",
        "A regression during the trial period — full refund.",
        "Any reason at all you decide it's not the right fit — full refund.",
      ],
